title: New York Fed Releases Special Report on the Future of New York City

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York released a special issue of the Economic Policy Review on October 11, 2023, featuring three academic papers from a March 2022 hybrid event titled 'The Future of New York City: Charting an Equitable Recovery for All.' The papers address urban economics topics including post-pandemic remote work trends and their effects on cities, office space demand, housing flexibility and conversions, and racial equity planning.
key_points: The publication is a special issue of the Economic Policy Review containing three research papers on urban economics
Topics covered include post-pandemic work location changes, office space demand, housing conversion flexibility, and racial equity planning
Authors include Gilles Duranton and Jessie Handbury, Ingrid Gould Ellen and Noah M. Kazis, and Lance Freeman
The research focuses on New York City economic recovery and urban development policy
This is not directly related to financial market structure or macro market catalysts
why_it_matters: This source is a research release focused on urban economics and city planning policy, not financial market structure or macro catalysts. The source does not discuss market plumbing, trading operations, interest rate policy, or economic indicators that would impact financial markets. It is relevant only to those studying urban economics or regional New York City economic development.
